titleStmt.title: Letter from Charles Murray to Royal College of Physicians of Ireland, 6 December 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Charles Murray

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from Charles Murray asking if the Royal College of Physicians of Ireland (RCPI) could possibly send two replacement copies of his diplomas, which he lost while working as a locum. Murray also expresses his regret upon hearing of the death of Dr White and extends his sympathy.
The letter has been initialled by Ephraim MacDowel Cosgrave (1853-1925). Cosgrave served as president of the Royal College of Physicians of Ireland in 1914 and held the position until 1916. Aside from his dedication to medicine Cosgrave was also a member of the Royal Society of Antiquities of Ireland, treasurer of the Royal Zoological Society of Ireland and a founder and secretary of the Georgian Society of Dublin.

msIdentifier.repository: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland

correspAction.placeName: Appleton House, Station Road, Hinckley, Leicestershire
Royal College of Physicians, Kildare Street, Dublin, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Regd 1914. July 30 J.
Deisagoland,
Virginia, Cavan Appleton House
Station Rd
Hinckley
Leicistershire VI/XII/XV Dear Sir, I am sorry to be thus forced to trouble
you. Some months ago in travelling around here
doing Locum my box in which were my
diplomas was lost. I have hopelessly failed
to find it. As I am settling here in a
practice I shall want a copy if it is
at all possible to procure some. As I shall need the copies in two weeks
time I hope you will please do what you
can in the matter. I am sorry to see by "B.M.J" that
Dear Dr White has passed away. You
shall miss him so much. Thanking you in anticipation & again
apologising for thus troubling you. I am Dear Sir
Yours Respectfully Charles Murray. MacdC.
7.I.16
